thesis: Pure-play U.S. solar domestic manufacturer building America's first fully integrated silicon solar supply chain. G1_Dallas facility operational (5 GW capacity), G2_Austin under construction ($400-425M investment, 2.1 GW cells). Section 45X manufacturing credits ($93M accrued) provide critical subsidy support. Recently turned EBITDA positive with improving margins. High leverage (269% D/E) and $34M restricted cash create balance sheet risk requiring additional financing.

drivers: Toliara FID approved (early 2026); Madagascar project adds REE/HMS diversification
G2_Austin comes online Q4 2026, creating fully integrated supply chain
First Solar valuation comparison drives multiple expansion

risks: Balance sheet stress forces highly dilutive financing
G1_Dallas fails to achieve 4.5 GW run rate

base_case.assumptions: Toliara FID delayed but proceeds; 45X credits partially monetized; G2 operational by Q4 2026; Revenue reaches $600M+ by 2027; EBITDA breakeven achieved; One additional equity raise required.

catalysts.event: Toliara Project FID — Major project sanctioning
Donald Project FID — JV with Astron (Australia)
G2_Austin Production Start — Fully integrated supply chain

key_risks: Critical balance sheet risk — 269% D/E, only $34M unrestricted cash
Policy dependency on 45X tax credits and domestic content requirements
Variable revenue due to utility contract timing
Intense competition from First Solar (profitable, $15B+ market cap)
